mainmatter.p: Let B \colon A \to \mathsf {Type} be a family of propositions; fixing f \colon \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } {Bx}, it suffices to show any other g \colon \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } Bx may be identified with f. As B is a family of propositions, we know that each Bx is contractible with centre fx. Therefore, the closure of contractible types under dependent products () shows that \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } Bx is contractible with centre f.
The dependent product of a family of propositions is a proposition.

A type A is said to be contractible with centre a:A when every x:A is equipped with an identification \chi _x \colon a = x. We say that A is contractible when it comes equipped with some a:A such that A is contractible with centre a. We sometimes refer to a contractible type as a singleton.

Given a dependent function f \colon \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } {Bx}, if each component Bx is contractible with centre fx then the dependent product \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } {Bx} is contractible with centre f.
This follows immediately from univalence for functions.
A type A is said to be a proposition when every x:A, the type A is contractible with centre x, i.e. all elements of A are identified.
